reasoning: Query asks specifically about GI-protected awamori and its characteristics/production areas. Response returns only one GI item (琉球もろみ酢) which is derived from awamori lees but is not awamori itself. The scrape items are mostly irrelevant (castle map, tourism nav pages) with only partial awamori text buried in body_paragraphs. RULE C: authoritative MAFF GI data present but the core entity (awamori as GI product) is absent from GI records — awamori is not itself GI-registered under MAFF, so the gap is structural. Scraped content does mention awamori characteristics. Coverage sparse (G).
missing_data: Awamori itself is not a MAFF GI product — this gap should be explained to the agent. No production area breakdown (e.g. Miyakojima, Yonaguni) or GI designation status clarification.
improvement_hint: Add a note when keyword matches a famous product not in GI records, suggesting the agent use search_hybrid or get_description for awamori-specific Wikidata/scrape data. Filter out nav-chrome scrape items with no food content.

reasoning: Query asks about tofu and shojin ryori vegetarian experiences in Kyoto. Tool called with keyword '豆腐' returning only 2 items, neither of which is about tofu cuisine — item 1 is a sightseeing bus tour narrative (mentions 湯豆腐膳 in passing), item 2 appears truncated. No shojin ryori data returned. RULE A: wrong keyword scope — '豆腐' in scraped_local_food returns tangential content. The agent needed vegetarian food data but retrieval substantially missed. Retrieval failure (A).
missing_data: Direct tofu restaurant/kaiseki listings, shojin ryori temples (Nanzenji, Daitokuji areas), specific tofu producers (Morika, Tousuiro). GI records for Kyoto food products.
improvement_hint: Broaden keyword matching for food queries — '豆腐' should also surface items tagged with 精進料理, 湯豆腐, 京料理. Consider a secondary GI lookup without keyword filter for Kyoto food when primary keyword returns <3 items.

reasoning: Query asks about 白エビ (white shrimp) and Toyama Black ramen. Response returns 135 items but top items are 入善ジャンボ西瓜 (watermelon) and 富山干柿 (persimmon) — not the iconic items the query specifically mentioned. Scraped item visible is Toyama City tourism general page. White shrimp (白エビ) is not GI-designated, so its absence in GI records is expected, but ranking buries relevant content. RULE B: data likely present in corpus but not surfaced at top. Authoritative Toyama food data present (MAFF GI items are real/accurate), scope just not matching query focus.
missing_data: 白エビ (white shrimp) specific content, 富山ブラック ramen cultural background. These may be in body_paragraphs of lower-ranked scraped items.
improvement_hint: Implement keyword-boosted ranking when explicit named products appear in query — '白エビ' and '富山ブラック' should score higher than generic GI items. Consider surfacing top scraped items that match query keywords even if GI records dominate the sort order.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks about Nara specialty foods and officially recognized agricultural products. Response returns only 1 GI item (三輪素麺) with Arabic translation — accurate and on-topic. However coverage is sparse: Nara has other known products (大和牛, 大和茶, 吉野葛) that may exist as GI or specialty items but are absent. see_also_wikidata_venues shows museums, not food venues, which is unhelpful. RULE C: authoritative data present but incomplete scope (G). Language constraint (Arabic) is handled — translation present.
missing_data: Other Nara GI or specialty food products beyond Miwa Somen. 大和茶, 大和牛, 吉野葛 if registered. Food-specific venue suggestions.
improvement_hint: For single-result GI responses, add a fallback to get_local_food without category filter to supplement coverage. Filter see_also_wikidata_venues by relevance to food queries.

reasoning: Query asks about 地鶏 (local chicken) and 宮崎牛 (Miyazaki beef) with GI characteristics. Response returns 3 GI items: 宮崎牛 (excellent, directly answers question), ヤマダイかんしょ (sweet potato), 米良糸巻大根 (daikon). 宮崎牛 data is detailed and accurate with competition history. However, 宮崎地鶏 (local chicken) — the other key product in the query — is not GI-registered under MAFF and is entirely absent. Coverage gap (G) for the explicitly named 地鶏. Data that is present is high quality and authoritative.
missing_data: 宮崎地鶏 (not MAFF GI but could appear in scraped data), explanation that 地鶏 is not GI-designated, comparison between 宮崎牛 and standard beef characteristics.
improvement_hint: When a query mentions a famous product not in GI records, add a supplementary scrape search or note explaining GI coverage limits. For 宮崎地鶏 specifically, municipal scrape likely has relevant content.

reasoning: Query asks about natto and fermented foods from Ibaraki with officially designated products. Tool called with keyword '発酵' (fermentation). Response returns 2 items: both are scraped community blog posts from Ryugasaki (a different area) about local events, completely unrelated to natto or fermented food GI products. Natto (納豆) is strongly associated with Ibaraki/Mito but does not appear. No GI records for Ibaraki fermented products are returned. Retrieval failure (A) — relevant data not fetched.
missing_data: 水戸納豆 or 茨城納豆 GI/specialty records if any. Ibaraki fermented food overview. Any MAFF GI records for Ibaraki.
wrong_data: Both returned items are irrelevant community blog posts about walking tours and theatrical groups — zero food content relevant to the query.
improvement_hint: Keyword '発酵' is too specialized and matches blog text about fermentation festivals elsewhere. Add fallback: if keyword returns <3 relevant items, retry without keyword and filter by GI category. Also index natto-related content explicitly.

reasoning: Query asks about 八丁味噌-based Aichi dishes (みそかつ, みそ煮込みうどん) and cultural background. Response returns 39 items with 八丁味噌 GI record as first item — highly accurate with production history, MAFF designation, two breweries (まるや, カクキュー) with visitor info. Scraped body_paragraphs provide rich cultural/historical context (武田信玄, Edo period, stone-weight method). Miso-based dishes mentioned in detail. The specific dishes 味噌カツ and 味噌煮込みうどん may appear in lower-ranked items. Overall strong response. Minor caveat: the query focus on specific dishes is addressed via scrape context rather than explicit dish listings.
missing_data: Direct listing of みそかつ and みそ煮込みうどん as distinct dishes with restaurant recommendations.
improvement_hint: Surface dish-level items (みそかつ, 味噌煮込みうどん) more prominently when query explicitly names them alongside the base ingredient GI product.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks about officially protected traditional Japanese confectionery (wagashi) across Japan. Tool called get_local_specialty with q='和菓子' and lang='id' returns count:0 items. The see_also_wikidata_venues shows completely irrelevant venues (Tomioka Silk Mill, Itsukushima, Kamakura Museum). The keyword '和菓子' does not match any GI records despite ちんすこう (Okinawa, GI#155) and 沖縄黒糖 (GI#172) being wagashi-related. RULE A: retrieval failure — needed data not fetched. No Indonesian translation present either. Complete failure.
missing_data: ちんすこう (GI#155), 沖縄黒糖 (GI#172), and any other confectionery GI items. Indonesian translations.
wrong_data: see_also_wikidata_venues items are entirely unrelated to wagashi.
improvement_hint: The query '和菓子' needs semantic expansion to match GI items tagged as 焼き菓子, 菓子, or confectionery. Add category synonym mapping so '和菓子' also queries items with 菓子 in description. The GI items R-191 found with q='菓子' (3 results) should also be findable with '和菓子'.

reasoning: Query asks about Hokkaido jingisukan culture, lamb origin/production areas, and traditional eating styles. Response returns 44 items. First item from Takikawa city official site provides excellent historical background: origin at Takikawa seed sheep farm, WWI wool-self-sufficiency policy, Takikawa-style vs Sapporo-style eating distinction, 1955 first public offering, 1956 Matsuo popularization, 2004 designation as Hokkaido Heritage. Accurate and detailed. Second item adds cherry blossom + jingisukan culture at Goryokaku. Good scrape data. Minor caveat: lamb production area detail (domestic vs imported) could be clearer.
missing_data: Specific domestic lamb production areas (e.g. Shizunai, Akan), imported vs domestic lamb distinction commonly used in Hokkaido restaurants.
improvement_hint: Lamb sourcing information (domestic Hokkaido farms vs. Australian/New Zealand imports) would strengthen practical usefulness for travelers wanting authentic local lamb.

reasoning: Query in Portuguese asks about Tottori specialties especially 松葉ガニ (Matsuba crab). Response returns 500 of 658 items (truncated) with Portuguese translations. GI items shown are 鳥取砂丘らっきょう (rakkyo), 大山ブロッコリー (broccoli), こおげ花御所柿 (persimmon), 大栄西瓜 (watermelon). Matsuba crab is notably absent — it is not a MAFF GI product under Tottori specifically (it's marketed as ズワイガニ across western Japan). Portuguese translations present. RULE D applied: language constraint handled. Coverage gap for the explicitly requested product (Matsuba crab). Structural absence rather than ranking failure.
missing_data: 松葉ガニ (Matsuba/Snow crab) — note it is not Tottori-specific GI but is the prefecture's most famous seafood. No seafood GI items for Tottori.
improvement_hint: For queries explicitly naming a famous non-GI product, add a note or supplementary scrape result. Tottori crab season and port information should be surfaced from municipal scrapes.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 鹿児島黒豚 (Kagoshima black pig) characteristics and branding history. Tool called get_local_specialty with q='黒豚' returns count:0 — no items. The see_also_wikidata_venues shows Kagoshima museums/cultural sites, completely irrelevant. 鹿児島黒豚 is not a MAFF GI product (it's a JA brand/registered trademark rather than GI designation), so structural absence in GI records. However, the agent received zero useful data about black pork at all. Complete retrieval failure.
missing_data: 鹿児島黒豚 brand history, バークシャー (Berkshire) breed characteristics, difference from regular pork. Scrape data from Kagoshima likely has this.
wrong_data: see_also_wikidata_venues items entirely unrelated to black pork.
improvement_hint: When GI query returns 0 items, auto-fallback to get_local_food with prefecture+keyword would likely surface black pork from municipal scrapes. Add routing hint in zero-result responses.

reasoning: Query asks about sake brewing in Fushimi, Kyoto and designated sake varieties. Response returns 6 items with keyword '日本酒'. First item is cherry blossom viewing guide that mentions 伏見 and sake ('利き酒') in passing — relevant context but not the primary focus. The item contains rich Fushimi historical context: 日本三大酒どころ, 伏見七名水, UNESCO 無形文化遺産 2024 registration. However, no specific GI designation for Fushimi sake appears. Fushimi sake is not a MAFF GI product. Data present but Fushimi sake appears as contextual mention rather than dedicated record. Ranking issue (B).
missing_data: Dedicated Fushimi/Kyoto sake records. GI designation status clarification (Fushimi is not MAFF GI). Major breweries (Gekkeikan, Kizakura, Fushimi brewery district overview).
improvement_hint: Cherry blossom viewing page ranks first for '日本酒' keyword — ranking should prioritize items where sake is the primary topic. Add brewery/sake-specific content from Fushimi municipal tourism pages.

reasoning: Query asks about Yamanashi's hoto dish and whether it is officially recognized as Koshu Basin food culture. Response returns 64 items, top item is a comprehensive Yamanashi tourism page with rich hoto content: cultural background (武田信玄 military ration origin), preparation methods, variations (silk-powder 甲斐絹ほうとう), ozara, restaurant recommendations across regions (Kofu, Katsunuma, Fuji Five Lakes, Fujikawa, Minobu). Accurate historical and culinary detail. Note: hoto is not a MAFF GI product — official recognition aspect partially addressed by cultural heritage context but not explicitly stated.
missing_data: Explicit statement about hoto's official designation status (it is registered as a nationally designated traditional dish by Ministry of Agriculture but not MAFF GI). Seasonal best time to eat hoto.
improvement_hint: Add a flag or note when a queried dish is nationally recognized but not MAFF GI — this is useful context for agents answering 'officially recognized' questions.

reasoning: Query in Russian asks about Osaka traditional dishes with official recognition (takoyaki, okonomiyaki, etc.). Response returns 460 items with Russian translations for GI items (富田林の海老芋, 泉州水なす). GI items are Osaka-specific and accurately translated. Scraped item '大阪のおすすめグルメ' mentions たこ焼き, お好み焼き, 串カツ in description — relevant. However, the query is in Russian but the scraped content body is entirely in Japanese (no Russian translation). RULE D applies: language mismatch for scraped content. GI items have Russian. Constraint partially handled (-1 for language). Overall coverage is reasonable but takoyaki/okonomiyaki are not GI-protected, creating a gap between what was asked and what GI records can provide.
missing_data: Explanation that たこ焼き and お好み焼き are not GI-protected. Russian-language content for scraped dishes. 串カツ cultural context.
improvement_hint: When query asks about famous dishes not in GI records, surface an advisory note. Russian translations should be applied to scraped content descriptions as well, not just GI items.

reasoning: Query asks about Echizen crab brand and fishing season, and how it is GI-certified. Response returns 1 GI item (越前がに, GI#69) with highly relevant data: quality characteristics (cold storage, fresh crab miso/naiko), 90+ year history of imperial household gift, national recognition, above-average pricing. MAFF source URL provided. Accurate. Minor caveat: fishing season (November–March) is not explicitly stated in the GI record text shown, which is the specific information the traveler needs most. see_also venues are unrelated to crab but do not mislead.
missing_data: Explicit fishing season dates (typically Nov 6 – Mar 20). Price ranges. The yellow tag (黄色いタグ) brand distinction. Port landing locations (越前町, 敦賀港).
improvement_hint: GI records could be supplemented with scraped fishery/tourism pages that include seasonal operation info. A structured 'season' field in GI records would directly address this common traveler query pattern.

reasoning: Query asks about regional Japanese confectionery traditions and officially designated wagashi across Japan. Response returns 3 GI items with keyword '菓子': やまえ栗 (Kumamoto chestnut, used in confectionery), ちんすこう (Okinawa traditional baked sweet, GI#155), 沖縄黒糖 (Okinawa black sugar, GI#172). All three are accurately described and relevant to confectionery. However, coverage is sparse — only 3 items nationwide, all Okinawa-adjacent, missing major wagashi regions (Kyoto, Kanazawa, Edo-period confectionery traditions). The keyword '菓子' is too narrow. RULE C: authoritative data present but incomplete scope (G).
missing_data: Kyoto confectionery traditions, Kanazawa 和菓子, other regional confectionery GI items. Explanation that most traditional wagashi are not individually GI-registered.
improvement_hint: Broaden '菓子' query to also match items with 'sweets', 'confection', 'dessert', '銘菓' in descriptions. Add coverage note when results appear geographically clustered (3/3 from Okinawa area).

reasoning: Query asks about peak bloom timing at Hirosaki Park and specifically the 花筏 (petal-floating moat) phenomenon with timing. Response returns 5 spots: 弘前公園 (Wikidata, sakura_meisho_100 tag, must_see tier) and municipal scrape items including a cherry blossom special feature page. 弘前公園 is correctly identified as the top spot. However, no bloom timing or 花筏 timing information is present in any returned item. The q_relevance scores are 0 for the Wikidata item. Practical usability limited because the core traveler question (when to see 花筏) cannot be answered from this data. Practicality failure (E).
missing_data: Bloom timing (typically late April), 花筏 phenomenon occurs ~1 week after peak bloom (early May), specific 2024/2025 forecast data. Festival dates (弘前さくらまつり).
improvement_hint: get_spots for cherry blossom queries should be supplemented with get_festivals or get_events for bloom-season event data. Adding a seasonal_notes field to spots with sakura_meisho_100 kind would directly answer timing questions.

reasoning: Query asks for best cherry blossom spots in Tokyo beyond Ueno Park. Tool uses search_hybrid with '桜 花見 東京'. Top result '春の東京' mentions Chidorigafuchi, Yoyogi Park, Inokashira Park, Showa Memorial Park, Nezu Shrine, Kameido Tenmangu — directly relevant. Second result is Tochigi prefecture cherry blossoms — off-target geographically. Third and fourth results are Musashimurayama (Tokyo suburb but not central). Results in English as requested. The top result is highly useful but subsequent results show ranking noise. RULE B: data present but some results buried below noise.
missing_data: Named specific spots like 千鳥ヶ淵 (Chidorigafuchi) with direct Wikidata entries, 目黒川 (Meguro River), 六義園. Shinjuku Gyoen.
improvement_hint: For cherry blossom spot queries, filter for prefecture_code:13 (Tokyo) more aggressively. The Tochigi result at rank 2 reduces usefulness. Prefecture-constrained hybrid search for Tokyo-specific queries would improve precision.

reasoning: Query asks about Kyoto autumn foliage avoiding crowds, spots beyond Arashiyama/Kiyomizudera. Top result is a cherry blossom page (桜 穴場) that also covers multiple Kyoto sub-areas including Nishikyo, Yamashina, Fushimi, Kitayama, Ohara, Takao — partially useful for geography but wrong season. Second result from Kyoto Tourism Federation has a 紅葉 calendar (autumn leaves) directly relevant. Third result (厭離庵) is a Kyoto autumn foliage spot. Fourth (紅葉谷) relevant. Fifth (紅葉山公園) is Fukushima, off-target. Overall the hybrid search surfaces some useful autumn foliage content with specific named spots. Query is in Japanese and results are in Japanese.
missing_data: Named lesser-known autumn spots with opening hours and access. Specific timing (typically early-mid November for Kyoto). Crowd levels for each spot.
wrong_data: Fifth result (紅葉山公園, Fukushima) is geographically off-target.
improvement_hint: Prefecture filter for Kyoto would eliminate the Fukushima false positive. Autumn foliage query should boost DMO calendar pages (like the Kyoto Tourism Federation result) over generic spring pages.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks about Hokkaido winter snow festivals beyond Sapporo Snow Festival. Response returns 3 canonical festivals in the canonical_festivals block: さっぽろ雪まつり, 旭川冬まつり (world's largest single-piece snow sculpture at Ishikari River — directly answers the 'beyond Sapporo' need), 小樽雪あかりの路 (canal candle-light festival). All three are accurately described with periods. However, all descriptions/names are in Japanese and English — no Korean translation despite lang='ko'. RULE D: language mismatch (-1 on constraint_handling). Core content is otherwise strong. The query_intent wrongly detected 'kaido' concept from Korean text.
missing_data: Korean translations of festival names and descriptions.
wrong_data: query_intent detected 'kaido' concept — this appears to be a false trigger from Korean romanization, not relevant to the query.
improvement_hint: Festival canonical entries should include Korean translations for name and description when lang='ko'. The intent classifier may be incorrectly parsing Korean characters — add language detection guard before concept matching.

reasoning: Query asks about best timing for autumn foliage in Nikko and most dramatic spots during koyo season. Response returns spots for Nikko municipality: 日光田母沢御用邸記念公園 (park, rekishi_park_100, opening hours present), 東武ワールドスクウェア (theme park — irrelevant to autumn foliage), イタリア大使館別荘記念公園 (park near lake Chuzenji — relevant for foliage), 日光江戸村 (Edo theme park — less relevant). Key Nikko foliage spots missing: 中禅寺湖, 竜頭の滝, いろは坂, 東照宮 surrounding trees. No timing information (mid-October to mid-November). Practicality failure (E) — response is correct but not directly useful for the foliage timing question.
missing_data: Peak foliage timing (October at Senjogahara, November at lower areas), 竜頭の滝, 中禅寺湖, いろは坂 as named foliage spots. Seasonal access restrictions.
improvement_hint: For autumn foliage queries at specific sites, supplement get_spots with get_festivals/get_events for autumn foliage events. Add seasonal_highlights field to known foliage destinations.

reasoning: Query asks about cherry blossoms + river rafting at Nagatoro and the season both can be enjoyed. Response returns 長瀞渓谷 (valley, sakura_meisho_100 and chishitsu_100 kinds, must_see tier) — directly relevant. Also 宝登山 (mountain), 宝登山神社 (shrine). Two municipal scrape items are noise (Kurashinobenrichou and a Saitama gift shop in Omiya). The key Wikidata item confirms Nagatoro Valley is a sakura_meisho_100 spot. However, no timing information about when sakura + rafting overlap (early April), no specific rafting operator information. Practicality failure (E) — correct but unusable for answering the timing/planning question.
missing_data: Cherry bloom timing at Nagatoro (late March-early April), rafting season overlap, 荒川ライン下り operator information, 長瀞岩畳 as a specific landmark.
wrong_data: 大宮駅の推しみやげ item is entirely off-topic (Omiya station souvenirs).
improvement_hint: Cherry blossom season queries should surface timing data. Nagatoro rafting (ライン下り) should be indexed as a specific activity. Filter out items with coordinates far from the queried municipality.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks about best time to see Mt. Fuji snow scenery and best Fuji Five Lakes photography angles. Response uses search_hybrid with '富士山 雪景' and lang='zh'. Top result is Yamagata tourism page (wrong prefecture entirely). Second result is Yamanashi 富士山 page in Chinese Traditional — highly relevant, mentions 富士五湖 and photography spots. Third result is Toyama tourism in Chinese — off-topic. Fourth result (富士山絶景ポイント) from Yaizu (Shizuoka) is relevant — mentions autumn-spring as best season. Fifth result is 富士登山 (climbing). RULE B: relevant Fuji content is present (rank 2, 4) but buried under Yamagata (rank 1) and Toyama (rank 3) noise.
missing_data: Specific best angles per lake (Kawaguchiko sunrise, Yamanakako reflection, Motosuko — the iconic 1000-yen bill view), winter season timing (December-February for snow cap).
improvement_hint: '富士山 雪景' search returns Yamagata at rank 1 due to snow content on that page — prefecture-aware filtering needed. Chinese query about Fuji should constrain to Yamanashi/Shizuoka prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku summer fireworks festivals, specifically whether 秋田竿燈まつり and fireworks can be enjoyed simultaneously. Tool called get_festivals with prefecture=Akita, keyword=花火. Response returns 0 local events, no canonical_festivals for Akita, but provides helpful routing_hint pointing to get_events for hanabi queries. The no_local_festivals_advisory message is clear. However, for an Akita query, the response should ideally surface 秋田竿燈まつり as a canonical festival even when keyword='花火'. The routing hint is useful but the agent receives no actual festival data for Akita. Retrieval failure (A) — needed data not fetched from corpus.
missing_data: 秋田竿燈まつり (August, UNESCO-listed), 大曲花火大会 (全国花火競技大会), Tohoku region hanabi overview.
improvement_hint: Akita's canonical festivals (竿燈まつり, 大曲花火大会) should appear in canonical_festivals block even when keyword filter yields 0 results. The canonical list should not be filtered by keyword.

reasoning: Query in French asks about best cherry blossom spots in Tohoku. Tool called get_spots with prefecture=Iwate, q=桜. Response returns 7 spots, but the top 3 are duplicates of the same municipal scrape item (桜情報2023ー県南エリア, same spot_id 8ba91fa74a8890f7 repeated with different municipalities). The response is single-prefecture (Iwate only) while the query asks about all of Tohoku. No French content. RULE D: language mismatch (French query, Japanese/no-language response). RULE A: retrieval failure on scope — Tohoku has famous Sendai/Hirosaki/Kakunodate cherry spots not covered.
missing_data: Cherry blossom spots in other Tohoku prefectures (Aomori/弘前, Akita/角館, Miyagi/西公園, Yamagata). French translations. Timing information.
wrong_data: Three identical duplicate entries for the same spot_id across different municipalities.
improvement_hint: Deduplicate spot_id entries in results. For a Tohoku-scope query, consider region fanout or at minimum suggest other Tohoku prefectures. Add French translations to scrape descriptions when lang='fr'.
